title: ds-viz-timeline - option matrix (dark)
description: Test bench for splunk.timeline. NOTE: splunk.timeline is in the validator's accepted enum but is NOT documented in the 10.4 reference PDF. Options below are verified empirically: category (DOS for the lane field), legendDisplay, backgroundColor, seriesColors, axisVisibility, showProgressBar/showLastUpdated. Drilldowns supported via primary.click.

options.query: | makeresults count=12 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24-rn*2) . "h") | eval category = case(rn%3==0,"deploy", rn%3==1,"alert", 1==1,"audit") | eval label = case(category=="deploy","web-" . tostring(rn) . " deployed", category=="alert","alert: cpu > 90% on host-" . tostring(rn), 1==1,"user_login on host-" . tostring(rn)) | eval severity = case(category=="alert",random()%3+1, 1==1,1) | table _time category label severity

ds_deploys_only.name: deploys only - one lane, simple timeline

options.query: | makeresults count=8 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(20-rn*2) . "h") | eval env = case(rn%3==0,"prod", rn%3==1,"staging", 1==1,"dev") | eval label = "deploy v1." . tostring(rn) | table _time env label

ds_attack_types.name: attack types over time - SOC investigation pattern

options.query: | makeresults count=15 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24-rn) . "h") | eval attack_type = case(rn%5==0,"phishing", rn%5==1,"brute_force", rn%5==2,"sql_injection", rn%5==3,"ddos", 1==1,"malware") | eval src_ip = "10.0.0." . tostring(rn) | eval count = rn | table _time attack_type src_ip count

ds_dense.name: dense events - tests visual clutter handling

options.query: | makeresults count=40 | streamstats count AS rn | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-" . tostring(24*60-rn*30) . "m") | eval category = case(rn%4==0,"info", rn%4==1,"warn", rn%4==2,"error", 1==1,"critical") | eval label = category . "_event_" . tostring(rn) | table _time category label

options.markdown:
# `splunk.timeline` - option matrix

The timeline visualization plots discrete events along a horizontal time axis, optionally grouped into lanes by a `category` field.

**Note**: `splunk.timeline` is NOT in the 10.4 reference PDF. Options below are verified empirically against Splunk Cloud 10.2.x. The viz is officially supported but its option surface is small and lightly documented.

Use it when:
- The story is **"when did things happen?"** rather than "how much".
- Events are sparse and discrete (deploys, alerts, audits) - not continuous metrics.
- Multiple lanes (`category`) help tell the story.

Use a column or area chart when events are dense and you care about counts.

viz_default.title: 1. Default - no options, single lane
viz_default.description: The bare minimum: a primary data source. The viz auto-derives the lane from the data and renders a single timeline.

viz_category.title: 2. category - lane by field via DOS
viz_category.description: Use `category` (Dynamic Options Syntax) to specify the lane field. The DOS expression `> primary | seriesByName('category')` resolves to the `category` column. Each unique value gets its own horizontal lane.

viz_legend_off.title: 3. legendDisplay: off
viz_legend_off.description: Hide the legend entirely. Useful when the lanes are self-explanatory or when screen real estate is tight.

viz_legend_bottom.title: 4. legendDisplay: bottom
viz_legend_bottom.description: Move the legend below the chart. Default position is `right`. Other valid values: `left`, `top`, `bottom`, `off`.

viz_custom_colors.title: 5. seriesColors - custom palette
viz_custom_colors.description: Override the auto-generated palette with `seriesColors`. The colors map by series order (alphabetical on the category field).

viz_bg_color.title: 6. backgroundColor - custom panel tint
viz_bg_color.description: Tint the events area with `backgroundColor`. Pairs well with bold series colors for editorial impact.

viz_axis_hidden.title: 7. axisVisibility: hide
viz_axis_hidden.description: Hide the time axis labels for a clean, decorative timeline. Works empirically; not in the 10.4 PDF but used in production dashboards.

viz_attack_types.title: 8. SOC pattern - attack types over time
viz_attack_types.description: Real-world pattern: attack categories on lanes, fed from a SOC investigation search. Use this layout for incident review and investigative dashboards.

viz_deploys.title: 9. Deploy timeline - env lanes
viz_deploys.description: Deploys grouped by environment lane. The `env` field becomes the lane separator.

viz_dense.title: 10. Dense events - 40 events stress test
viz_dense.description: Stress test with 40 events across 4 categories. Demonstrates how the viz handles overlapping markers - they cluster but remain interactive.

viz_no_title.description: No title set - the timeline is a section element rather than a panel. Pair with a markdown header for editorial layouts.
